annotations.note: `StrongDual R M` abbreviates `M →L[R] R`, the space of continuous linear functionals.

annotations.label: Algebraic dual space

anchor.snippet: is defined as the set of all linear maps

annotations.note: `Module.Dual R M = M →ₗ[R] R` is exactly the set of all linear maps to the base ring.

annotations.label: Natural pairing

anchor.snippet: called the natural pairing

annotations.note: The pairing ⟨φ,x⟩ = φ(x) is given by `Module.Dual.eval` (with `eval_apply`) / the identity bilinear map on `Dual R M`.

annotations.note: Coordinate functionals `Basis.coord`/`dualBasis` exist, but no equivalence `Dual R (ι →₀ R) ≃ (ι → R)` for the infinite-dimensional identification was found.

annotations.label: Dual of infinite-dimensional space has larger dimension

anchor.snippet: then its (algebraic) dual space is always of larger dimension

annotations.note: `lift_rank_lt_rank_dual` states the rank of an infinite-dimensional space is strictly less than that of its dual.

annotations.label: Erdős–Kaplansky theorem

anchor.snippet: The exact dimension of the dual is given by the Erdős–Kaplansky theorem

annotations.note: The Erdős–Kaplansky theorem is stated as `rank_dual_eq_card_dual_of_aleph0_le_rank`.

annotations.label: V isomorphic to V* but not naturally

anchor.snippet: But there is in general no natural isomorphism between these two spaces.

annotations.note: `Basis.toDualEquiv` gives `M ≃ₗ[R] Dual R M` from a finite basis; the 'no natural isomorphism' caveat is informal/not formalized.

annotations.label: Nondegenerate bilinear form induces isomorphism into dual

anchor.snippet: If the bilinear form is nondegenerate , then this is an isomorphism onto a subspace of

annotations.note: `BilinForm.toDual` builds `V ≃ₗ[K] Module.Dual K V` from a nondegenerate form on a finite-dimensional space.

annotations.label: Correspondence: isomorphisms into dual vs nondegenerate bilinear forms

anchor.snippet: Thus there is a one-to-one correspondence between isomorphisms of

annotations.note: Both directions exist (a bilinear form is a map to the dual, and `BilinForm.toDual` gives the iso), but the explicit bijective correspondence is not a single declaration.

annotations.label: Sesquilinear form gives isomorphism with conjugate dual

anchor.snippet: it is more natural to consider sesquilinear forms instead of bilinear forms

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace.toDual` is the conjugate-linear (sesquilinear) isometric iso to the dual for Hilbert spaces; the general nondegenerate-sesquilinear-form statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Natural map into the double dual

anchor.snippet: There is a natural homomorphism

annotations.note: `Module.Dual.eval R M : M →ₗ[R] Dual R (Dual R M)` is the canonical map into the double dual.

annotations.label: Double-dual map injective, iso iff finite-dimensional

anchor.snippet: and it is always an isomorphism if

annotations.note: `eval_apply_injective` gives injectivity over a field and `Module.evalEquiv` is the iso for reflexive (incl. finite-dimensional) spaces.

annotations.label: Transpose of a linear map

anchor.snippet: then the transpose (or dual )

annotations.note: `LinearMap.dualMap` (= `Module.Dual.transpose`) is the transpose `Dual R M₂ →ₗ[R] Dual R M₁`.

annotations.label: Identity characterizing the transpose

anchor.snippet: This identity characterizes the transpose

annotations.note: `transpose_apply`/`LinearMap.dualMap_apply` state `(transpose u) l = l.comp u`, i.e. ⟨ᵗf φ, x⟩ = ⟨φ, f x⟩.

annotations.label: Transpose assignment: injective, iso iff finite-dim, contravariant functor

anchor.snippet: produces an injective linear map between the space of linear operators

annotations.note: Contravariant functoriality `transpose_comp` is formalized, but the injectivity and finite-dimensional iso of the assignment `u ↦ ᵗu` are not stated together.

annotations.label: Transpose represented by transpose matrix

anchor.snippet: is represented by the transpose matrix

annotations.note: `LinearMap.toMatrix_transpose` shows the matrix of the transpose in dual bases is the transpose matrix.

annotations.label: Annihilator of a subset

annotations.note: `Submodule.dualAnnihilator W` is the submodule of functionals vanishing on `W`.

annotations.label: Continuous and algebraic dual coincide in finite dimensions

anchor.snippet: the continuous dual and the algebraic dual coincide

annotations.label: Continuous dual space

anchor.snippet: is defined as the space of all continuous linear functionals

annotations.note: `StrongDual R M = M →L[R] R` is the space of continuous linear functionals.

annotations.label: Strong topology

anchor.snippet: is the topology of uniform convergence on bounded subsets in

annotations.label: Strong topology is normed for normed spaces

annotations.note: The operator norm makes `E →SL F` (hence the strong dual) a normed space, and it is complete (Banach) when the codomain is complete.

annotations.note: The duals of `ℓ^1`, `c`, and `c_0` are not identified in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Riesz representation theorem (Hilbert dual)

anchor.snippet: By the Riesz representation theorem , the continuous dual of a Hilbert space is again a Hilbert space

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace.toDual` is the Fréchet–Riesz conjugate-linear isometric equivalence `E ≃ₗᵢ⋆[𝕜] StrongDual 𝕜 E` for a Hilbert space.

annotations.label: Riesz–Markov–Kakutani representation theorem

anchor.snippet: By the Riesz–Markov–Kakutani representation theorem , the continuous dual of certain spaces of continuous functions can be described using measures.

annotations.note: `RealRMK.integral_rieszMeasure` represents a positive linear functional on `C_c(X,ℝ)` as integration against `rieszMeasure`.

annotations.label: Double-dual map injective for Hausdorff locally convex TVS

anchor.snippet: is Hausdorff and locally convex

annotations.note: Injectivity of the double-dual map for normed spaces follows from `inclusionInDoubleDualLi`; for Hausdorff locally convex TVS the separating-dual property (`SeparatingDual.exists_ne_zero`) gives it, but it is not stated as a double-dual injectivity result.
